argument_reference.name: ip_address_id
argument_reference.description: The IP address ID for which to create the port forwards. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

argument_reference.name: managed
argument_reference.description: USE WITH CAUTION! If enabled all the port forwards for this IP address will be managed by this resource. This means it will delete all port forwards that are not in your config! (defaults false)

argument_reference.name: project
argument_reference.description: The name or ID of the project to create this port forward in. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

argument_reference.name: forward
argument_reference.description: Can be specified multiple times. Each forward block supports fields documented below. The forward block supports:

argument_reference.name: protocol
argument_reference.description: The name of the protocol to allow. Valid options are: tcp and udp.

argument_reference.name: private_port
argument_reference.description: The private port to forward to.

argument_reference.name: public_port
argument_reference.description: The public port to forward from.

argument_reference.name: virtual_machine_id
argument_reference.description: The ID of the virtual machine to forward to.

argument_reference.name: vm_guest_ip
argument_reference.description: The virtual machine IP address for the port forwarding rule (useful when the virtual machine has secondairy NICs or IP addresses).

attributes_reference.name: id
attributes_reference.description: The ID of the IP address for which the port forwards are created.

attributes_reference.name: vm_guest_ip
attributes_reference.description: The IP address of the virtual machine that is used for the port forwarding rule.
